attributes.name: tagName
attributes.usage: The label users see when applying the tag. Drives autocomplete, search, and display in record sidebars. Should be short and meaningful — typically 1-3 words.

attributes.description: Free-form description of what the tag means and when to use it.
attributes.name: description
attributes.usage: Helps admins document tag meaning so multiple users apply tags consistently. Typically not shown when applying tags but visible in tag-management UI. Often empty in practice.

attributes.description: Self-referential lookup to a parent tag, enabling hierarchical classification.
attributes.name: parent
attributes.usage: Lets admins build tag hierarchies like "Healthcare > Pediatrics > NICU" or "Donor Type > Major Gift > Recurring." Hierarchy supports drill-down filtering (find all records tagged with anything under Healthcare) and aggregation. Not all systems support this.

attributes.description: Display color for the tag (hex code or named color).
attributes.name: color
attributes.usage: Visual differentiation in record displays. EspoCRM and Attio support tag colors natively. CiviCRM has color via the Tag's "color" field. Pure cosmetic — drives recognizability, not functionality.

attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the tag is available for assignment.
attributes.name: isActive
attributes.usage: Lets admins retire a tag without deleting it (preserving historical records that have the tag). Inactive tags hide from the apply-tag UI but remain on records that already have them.

attributes.description: System-generated timestamp of when the tag was created.
attributes.name: createdAt
attributes.usage: Standard audit field. Used in tag-administration reports.

business_context: Tag records are how CRMs handle classification that doesn't fit neatly into a structured field. Tags differ from enum-typed properties in that they're typically (1) polymorphic — one tag like "VIP" can be applied to Accounts, Contacts, Opportunities, and Donations alike; (2) hierarchical — tags can have parent-child relationships ("Healthcare > Pediatrics"); (3) admin-extensible — non-developers can add new tags without changing schema; (4) many-to-many — a record can have many tags simultaneously. This is the most architecturally divergent of the universal Tier 1 entities. CiviCRM, EspoCRM, Attio, and Bloomerang treat tags as first-class entities with their own management UI. Salesforce uses Topics (which are tag-like but technically different) plus Multi-select picklists (which are field-like). HubSpot has no first-class tag entity at all — what users call "tags" are typically multi-select properties or Lists. Different terms are used: Tags (Attio, EspoCRM, CiviCRM, Bloomerang uses Tag-like Groups), Topics (Salesforce), Categories (some systems), Labels (some systems). When interviewing clients, surface "Tag" by asking what categories they need to apply to records that are flexible, ad hoc, and may apply to several different kinds of records: VIP donor, prospect-stage, holiday-mailer-list, board-member, urgent-follow-up, language-preference. If they need this kind of cross-cutting classification, the target backend's tag-modeling capability matters significantly — it dictates whether tags can be re-used across entities or must be implemented as parallel custom fields per entity.
name: Tag
purpose: A cross-cutting classification label that can be attached to records of multiple entity types. Distinct from a structured field (which lives on a specific entity and has a fixed enum) — Tags are typically polymorphic, hierarchical, and admin-extensible without schema changes.

relationships.description: A tag can be applied to many accounts; an account can have many tags. Junction record name varies by system (TopicAssignment in Salesforce, EntityTag in CiviCRM).
A tag can be applied to many contacts; a contact can have many tags.
A tag can have a parent tag (hierarchical classification).

sources.title: HubSpot: Multi-select properties (closest equivalent to tags)
Attio: Create and manage attributes (multi-select for tag-style)
Bloomerang REST API documentation: Tag endpoint

systems.api_name: tags (as a multi-select attribute on each object)
systems.name: Tag (multi-select attribute)
systems.notes: Attio supports Tag-style attributes via multi-select attributes on objects. Tags are admin-defined per object (so a Companies object and a People object would have separate sets of Tag values) but management is straightforward.

systems.name: Tag (on Constituents)
systems.notes: Bloomerang has Tag as a first-class entity for Constituent classification. Tags can be hierarchical (categories of tags). Used heavily for donor segmentation, communication preferences, and affinity tracking.

systems.name: Tag
systems.notes: First-class Tag entity. CiviCRM Tags are polymorphic across Contact, Activity, Case, Contribution, and other entities. Supports a parent_id for hierarchy. Tags are organized into Tag Sets (categories of tags) for management. Distinct from Groups, which are saved searches / mailing lists rather than classification labels.

systems.name: Tag
systems.notes: EspoCRM has a first-class Tag entity (introduced for the global tagging feature). Tags are polymorphic — applied to any entity that has tagging enabled. Hierarchy is supported via parent tags. Standard entity available across all installations.

systems.name: Multi-select property (no first-class tag)
systems.notes: MAJOR DIVERGENCE. HubSpot has no first-class Tag entity. What users call "tags" are implemented as Multi-select properties (one per object that needs them) or as static Lists (which are saved filters). Cross-object tagging requires duplicate properties on each object — a "VIP" tag on Contact and a separate "VIP" multi- select option on Company.

systems.name: Topic / Multi-select picklist
systems.notes: MAJOR DIVERGENCE. Salesforce has Topics (admin-extensible labels that can be applied to records) and TopicAssignment (the junction record). Topics are technically polymorphic (any object enabled for Topics can have them) but the framework is less prominent than Multi-select Picklist fields, which most admins use when "tags" come up. Customers often ask for and don't get true tags.

systems.name: Topic / Multi-select picklist (same as Salesforce)
systems.notes: Same as Salesforce. NPSP doesn't add tag-specific functionality. Nonprofit deployments often build custom segmentation via Multi-select picklists on Contact (Donor Type, Affinity Areas, Communication Preferences) since tags-as-Topics are rarely used.
